# Memory

Marvin proposes updates as `[PROPOSED]` bullets in `_system/memory/daily/{date}.md`. After human review and discussion, promote approved items here.

**Do not edit this file during agent sessions** unless the human explicitly requests promotion (as on 2026-05-21).

Reference library: `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/` (Munger, Pabrai, Stahl PDFs).

**Last human promotion:** 2026-05-21 — wisdom beliefs + **8697.T** company deep dive.

---

## Approved beliefs — Charlie Munger

*Approved 2026-05-21*

### Mental models & latticework

- Build a latticework of mental models from multiple disciplines before specializing in stock picking; isolated facts without models produce "confusion and folly." — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/munger/Munger-1994-Elementary-Worldly-Wisdom.pdf`

### Inversion & failure avoidance

- Before asking "why is this a good investment," ask "what would make this fail?" and whether we can survive being wrong. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/munger/Munger-1994-Elementary-Worldly-Wisdom.pdf`

### Psychology of misjudgment

- Maintain a checklist of ~25 standard causes of human misjudgment; watch for **lollapalooza** effects when several biases combine. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/munger/Psychology-of-Human-Misjudgment.pdf`
- Incentive-caused bias is among the most reliable forces in human behavior — always map who gets paid for what before trusting management narrative. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/munger/Psychology-of-Human-Misjudgment.pdf`

### Moats & competitive advantage

- Prefer businesses where rational managers can defend pricing power over long periods; moat quality matters more than near-term EPS noise. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/munger/Munger-1994-Elementary-Worldly-Wisdom.pdf`

### Circle of competence & simplicity

- If we cannot explain the business in plain language within five sentences, we are outside circle of competence — pass or defer. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/munger/Munger-1994-Elementary-Worldly-Wisdom.pdf`

### Capital allocation & patience

*(No entries promoted yet — add from future deep dives.)*

## Approved beliefs — Mohnish Pabrai

*Approved 2026-05-21*

### Dhando & asymmetric payoffs

- Seek **heads I win, tails I don't lose much** — downside protection matters more than upside precision. — Dhando framework; `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/pabrai/` letters 2005–2020

### Bet sizing & concentration

- Run a concentrated portfolio of high-conviction ideas; redemptions are a feature when partners leave capital with fewer, better ideas. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/pabrai/Pabrai-Letter-l_010112.pdf` (2012)
- When subscriptions lag redemptions but idea quality is high, treat cash scarcity as a signal to prioritize — not to dilute into marginal names. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/pabrai/Pabrai-Letter-l_010124.pdf` (Jan 2024)

### Business selection

- Invest only in simple, understandable businesses with durable economics and honest management — skip complex stories requiring heroic assumptions. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/pabrai/` partner letters 2005–2025

### Cloning & learning

- Cloning great investors is valid starting point — but every cloned idea still requires independent primary-source verification before capital. — Dhando / Pabrai letter practice

### Valuation heuristics

*(No entries promoted yet — add from future deep dives.)*

### Portfolio discipline

- Wait for fat pitches; annual redemption windows and fund capacity limits are constraints to respect, not obstacles to force trades. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/pabrai/Pabrai-Letter-l_010125.pdf` (Jan 2025)

---

## Approved beliefs — Murray Stahl

*Approved 2026-05-21*

### Croupier business model

- **Croupiers** facilitate pecuniary transactions (exchanges, custodians, processors) without putting principal capital at risk — distinct from balance-sheet financials like banks. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/stahl/Stahl-Croupier-Business-Model-2008.pdf`
- Modern regulation pushes OTC/exotic instruments toward organized exchanges and clearing — expanding croupier TAM over time. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/stahl/Stahl-Croupier-Business-Model-2008.pdf`
- Liquidity demand can **rise** in crises (ETF turnover, futures hedging) even when credit markets freeze — do not assume croupier volumes collapse with recession. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/stahl/Stahl-Croupier-Business-Model-2008.pdf`

### Exchanges & market infrastructure

- For exchanges, analyze **handled volume** alongside market share — share loss and volume growth are not mutually exclusive. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/stahl/Stahl-Exchanges-Less-Talk-More-Figures-2009.pdf`
- Exchange operating margins can remain extraordinarily high (20–75%+) through severe market stress — durability matters more than one-year EPS dips. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/stahl/Stahl-Exchanges-Less-Talk-More-Figures-2009.pdf`
- Fee model matters: ad valorem pricing ties revenue to market levels; per-trade/volume pricing can be more resilient in drawdowns. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/stahl/Stahl-Exchanges-Less-Talk-More-Figures-2009.pdf`

### Real diversification

- ~50% of S&P 500 names are statistically irrelevant to index performance (≤7 bp weight each); index "diversification" is narrower than it appears. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/stahl/Stahl-Achievement-of-Diversification-2020.pdf`
- Low index weight ≠ low economic importance — compare employees, revenue, and real-economy footprint, not cap-weight alone. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/stahl/Stahl-Achievement-of-Diversification-2020.pdf`

### Spinoffs & corporate simplification

- Spinoffs and separations can unlock value when conglomerate complexity hides segment economics — screen for forced simplification events. — `_system/reference/investment-wisdom/stahl/Stahl-Spinoffs-Going-Separate-Ways-2015.pdf`

### Macro & pecuniary society

*(Covered under croupier TAM bullet above.)*

### Long product lifecycle & durability

*(No entries promoted yet — add from future deep dives.)*
